what:
An OpenFn adaptor for use with Mailchimp. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Mailchimp Marketing API.

It exposes 15 operations that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. 19 ready-to-paste snippets are grouped as create (11) and search (8). Vendor homepage: https://mailchimp.com/developer/.
connect:
Mailchimp (Intuit) supports API keys or OAuth 2.0 for API access.

The credential schema asks for server (required) and apiKey (required). Point the credential at the base URL: https://{dc}.api.mailchimp.com/3.0. Auth reference: https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/guides/quick-start/.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Mailchimp Marketing API.

7 triggers are catalogued (3 webhook, 4 polling), including Link Clicked, New Audience, New Campaign, New Customer, and more. See the Triggers tab for the full list. Mailchimp (Intuit) can push events to OpenFn via webhooks (docs: https://mailchimp.com/developer/marketing/guides/sync-audience-data-webhooks/), so reactive workflows are a good fit.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Kick the workflow off from a trigger on the Triggers tab (webhook-driven where supported, polling otherwise). Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Mailchimp (Intuit). Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Mailchimp (Intuit) jobs at volume:

- Prefer Mailchimp (Intuit) webhooks over polling — they cut latency and rate-limit pressure.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Data center (dc) is derived from the API key suffix.
best:
- Store Mailchimp (Intuit) credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-mailchimp to v1.0.31 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
